mechanism_narrative: A lower federal funds rate level directly reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, causing an immediate upward repricing of the S&P 500 over the subsequent 20 trading days as an always-on cross-sectional signal.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.15% as of Jan 2009 reflecting aggressive post-crisis easing
failure_condition: Spearman correlation between FEDFUNDS level and 20-day forward returns is positive over any 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 7 percent signals slack labor demand and collapsing household income expectations, causing equity risk premia to expand and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the threshold is breached.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE reached 7.6% in Jan 2009, breaching the 7% threshold
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of months when UNRATE exceeds 7.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Declining real retail sales concurrent with a federal funds rate below 0.25 percent indicates a demand collapse that monetary easing fails to arrest; this joint condition predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days because earnings revisions overwhelm valuation support.
evidence_refs: fact:Jan 2009 RSAFS fell sharply while FEDFUNDS was 0.15%
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of instances when RSAFS 3-month change is negative and FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month improvement in the 3-month rate of change of industrial production predicts positive S&P 500 returns only when the unemployment rate exceeds 6 percent, because during high-unemployment periods an industrial stabilization signals the early phase of a cyclical rebound rather than a late-cycle overheat.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.6% and INDPRO declining through Dec 2008
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 6.0%, months with INDPRO month-over-month change above -0.5% do not produce positive 20-day returns in at least 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative three-month decline in housing starts exceeding 10 percent signals sustained residential investment collapse and construction employment losses, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 550k in Dec 2008, down sharply from prior year
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of quarters triggered by HOUST declining more than 10% over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: A lower level of nonfarm payroll employment relative to its trailing 12-month maximum reflects cumulative labor income destruction, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as an always-on level signal.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 134580k in Jan 2009, down sharply from peak
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS level and 5-day forward returns is positive over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation accelerating above 3 percent annualized over 3 months while the federal funds rate remains below 1 percent produces deeply negative real interest rates that distort capital allocation and compress corporate margins, jointly predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of quarters when CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds 3% and FEDFUNDS is below 1%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 1 percent signals acute manufacturing contraction and inventory liquidation, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in more than 50% of months when INDPRO month-over-month change is below -1.0%.
